data.content:
--- ## TPS-Alpha (Next.js) Complete Application Map --- ### 1. ROUTE & PAGE STRUCTURE **Root Level:** - [app/page.tsx](app/page.tsx) → `/` — **Dashboard General** — displays key metrics from the ERP (articles, BOMs, projects, GAPs, LMAT 2.0 specs, ECNs, kits, validations) **Articulos Section** (Inventory/Articles): - [app/articulos/page.tsx](app/articulos/page.tsx) → `/articulos` — **Article Catalog** — searchable, filterable table of 30 sample articles with status, ABC class, supplier, price, completeness tracking - [app/articulos/nuevo/page.tsx](app/articulos/nuevo/page.tsx) → `/articulos/nuevo` — **Create New Article** — form to add article with UdM, supplier, pricing, warehouse, production routing - [app/articulos/importar/page.tsx](app/articulos/importar/page.tsx) → `/articulos/importar` — **Import Articles** — CSV importer with template download and validation - [app/articulos/\[id\]/page.tsx](app/articulos/[id]/page.tsx) → `/articulos/:id` — **Article Detail** — full article view with documents, suppliers, conversions, documents, quality checks **LMAT 2.0 Section** (Bill of Materials & Technical Specs): - [app/lmat/page.tsx](app/lmat/page.tsx) → `/lmat` — **LMAT Dashboard** — overview of BOMs, specifications, ECNs, supply kits, validations, where-used, with quick stats - [app/lmat/boms/page.tsx](app/lmat/boms/page.tsx) → `/lmat/boms` — **BOMs List** — view all BOMs by maturity status (en_desarrollo, preliminar, estabilizado, liberado, obsoleto) with health % and revision tracking - [app/lmat/boms/nuevo/page.tsx](app/lmat/boms/nuevo/page.tsx) → `/lmat/boms/nuevo` — **Create BOM** — hierarchical BOM builder with drag-drop component tree, cell assignment, operation mapping - [app/lmat/boms/\[id\]/page.tsx](app/lmat/boms/[id]/page.tsx) → `/lmat/boms/:id` — **BOM Detail & Edit** — full BOM editor with hierarchical component management, revision history, diff tracking - [app/lmat/especificaciones/page.tsx](app/lmat/especificaciones/page.tsx) → `/lmat/especificaciones` — **Technical Specifications** — manage armor levels, vehicle models, specs linked to BOMs - [app/lmat/especificaciones/\[id\]/page.tsx](app/lmat/especificaciones/[id]/page.tsx) → `/lmat/especificaciones/:id` — **Specification Detail** — view/edit technical specs with production processes, departments validation - [app/lmat/ecn/page.tsx](app/lmat/ecn/page.tsx) → `/lmat/ecn` — **Engineering Change Notices** — ECN workflow (solicitud → analisis → aprobacion → aplicacion → completado) - [app/lmat/ecn/\[id\]/page.tsx](app/lmat/ecn/[id]/page.tsx) → `/lmat/ecn/:id` — **ECN Detail** — manage ECN approvals by department, apply changes to affected BOMs - [app/lmat/kits/page.tsx](app/lmat/kits/page.tsx) → `/lmat/kits` — **Supply Kits** — manage material kits for cutting/assembly with item scanning, backorder tracking - [app/lmat/kits/\[id\]/page.tsx](app/lmat/kits/[id]/page.tsx) → `/lmat/kits/:id` — **Kit Detail** — full kit management with component list, scanned items tracking - [app/lmat/validacion/page.tsx](app/lmat/validacion/page.tsx) → `/lmat/validacion` — **Department Validation** — multi-department sign-off process for BOMs (pendiente → en_revision → completado → rechazado) - [app/lmat/where-used/page.tsx](app/lmat/where-used/page.tsx) → `/lmat/where-used` — **Where-Used Analysis** — find all BOMs/projects using a given article **Proyectos Section** (Project Management): - [app/proyectos/page.tsx](app/proyectos/page.tsx) → `/proyectos` — **Projects Dashboard** — active projects view with status stages, revenue, margin tracking, days in production - [app/proyectos/nuevo/page.tsx](app/proyectos/nuevo/page.tsx) → `/proyectos/nuevo` — **Create Project** — new project form with client, quotation, armor level, BOM selection - [app/proyectos/\[id\]/page.tsx](app/proyectos/[id]/page.tsx) → `/proyectos/:id` — **Project Detail & Traceability** — full project view with status pipeline (cotizacion → anticipo → pedido_firme → planeacion → produccion → calidad → facturacion → cobro → entregado), costs, documents **Implementacion Section** (ERP Implementation Tracking): - [app/implementacion/page.tsx](app/implementacion/page.tsx) → `/implementacion` — **Implementation Dashboard** — GAP tracking, checklist progress, decision status, article/BOM completeness metrics, by-area breakdown - [app/implementacion/gaps/page.tsx](app/implementacion/gaps/page.tsx) → `/implementacion/gaps` — **GAPs List** — implementation gaps by type (datos_maestros, automatizacion, reporte, workflow, integracion), priority, and area with resolution tracking - [app/implementacion/decisiones/page.tsx](app/implementacion/decisiones/page.tsx) → `/implementacion/decisiones` — **Decisions Log** — pending/decided items requiring architectural or process decisions --- ### 2. COMPONENTS OVERVIEW #### **Layout Components** ([components/layout/](components/layout/)) - [**app-sidebar.tsx**](components/layout/app-sidebar.tsx) — Collapsible navigation sidebar with 5 main groups (Inicio, LMAT, Articulos, Proyectos, Implementacion); group expansion state management; highlights current path - Props: `None` (uses `usePathname()` and `useStore()`) - [**header.tsx**](components/layout/header.tsx) — Top bar with: welcome message, user role selector, plant filter (todas/planta_1/planta_2/almacen_servicios), reset data button - Props: `None` (uses `useStore()` for dispatch, state) #### **Shared Components** ([components/shared/](components/shared/)) - [**stat-card.tsx**](components/shared/stat-card.tsx) — Reusable KPI card with label, value, subtitle, icon, optional trend indicator - Props: `label`, `value` (string|number), `subtitle?`, `icon?`, `trend?` ({ value, positive }), `className?` - [**status-badge.tsx**](components/shared/status-badge.tsx) — Badge for status display (articles, BOMs, projects, GAPs) with color class - Props: `label`, `colorClass`, `className?` #### **BOM Components** ([components/bom/](components/bom/)) - [**bom-tree.tsx**](components/bom/bom-tree.tsx) — Hierarchical tree renderer for BOM components with: - Flat-to-tree conversion (`parentId`-based hierarchy) - Drag-drop reordering via `onReorder` callback - Expand/collapse toggles - Visual distinction for kits vs regular components - Icons: GripVertical (drag), ChevronRight/Down (expand), Package/Boxes, AlertTriangle (incomplete), CheckCircle2 (complete) - Props: `components` (BOMComponent[]), `onReorder()`, `readOnly?` #### **UI Primitives** ([components/ui/](components/ui/)) (shadcn-ui based — 40+ components): - accordion, alert-dialog, alert, aspect-ratio, avatar, badge, breadcrumb, button-group, button, calendar - card, carousel, chart, checkbox, collapsible, command, context-menu, dialog, drawer, dropdown-menu - empty, field, form, hover-card, input-group, input-otp, input, item, kbd, label, menubar - navigation-menu, pagination, popover, progress, radio-group, resizable, scroll-area, select - separator, sheet, sidebar, skeleton, slider, sonner, spinner, switch, table, tabs, textarea - toast, toaster, toggle-group, toggle, tooltip, use-mobile, use-toast #### **Theme Provider** ([components/theme-provider.tsx](components/theme-provider.tsx)) - Wraps `next-themes` ThemeProvider for dark/light mode toggle - Props: `children`, `...ThemeProviderProps` --- ### 3. LIB/ FOLDER CONTENTS #### **[types.ts](lib/types.ts)** — Core Domain Models **Enums & Types:** - `UserRole` — "admin" | "gerente" | "key_user" | "operador" | "consultor" - `Plant` — "planta_1" | "planta_2" | "almacen_servicios" - `ArticleStatus` — "borrador" | "pendiente" | "activo" | "inactivo" - `ABCClass` — "A" | "B" | "C" | "sin_clasificar" - `BOMMaturityStatus` — "en_desarrollo" | "preliminar" | "estabilizado" | "liberado" | "obsoleto" - `BOMStatus` — "borrador" | "en_revision" | "aprobado" | "obsoleto" - `ProjectStatus` — 10-stage lifecycle (cotizacion → entregado/cancelado) - `GAPStatus`, `GAPType`, `GAPPriority`, `UdM`, `ArmorLevel`, `ArticleType`, `DocumentType`, `ProductionProcess`, `DepartmentValidationStatus`, `ECNStatus` **Core Entities:** - **User** — id, name, email, role, area, plant, avatarInitials - **Article** — ~30 fields covering 3 dimensions: - **Ingenieria**: code, description, descriptionGeneric, articleType (compra|fabricacion|ambas), UdM (base/BOM/purchase/storage/production), drawingRef, group, family - **Compras**: supplierId, supplierName, price, currency, suppliers[], leadTimeDays, multi-vendor support - **Almacen**: location, warehouse, minStock, maxStock, reorderPoint, supplyMethod (bom_kit|kanban|solicitud), requiresLotSerial - **Produccion**: route, workCenter, productionRoute[], subBOMId, cncProgramId - **Calidad**: qualityGroup, inspectionRequired, qualityApproved - **Sistema**: abcClass, status, completeness (0-100 %), conversions[], documents[], imageUrl, plant, p5Code (legacy code), audit (createdAt, updatedAt, createdBy) - **BOMComponent** (hierarchical, flat storage with parent/child links): - articleId, articleCode, articleDescription, quantity, udm, cell, operation, notes, hasCompleteData - **Hierarchy**: parentId (null=root), order, level, isKit - **BOMRevision** — version tracking with diff snapshots - **BOM**: - specificationCode, vehicleModel, armorLevel, plant, version, status, maturityStatus - components[] (hierarchical), revisions[], healthPercent (% components complete) - **LMAT 2.0**: departmentValidations[] (multi-dept sign-off), specificationId (link), plantRoutes[], cuttingKitIds[] - **Project**: - folioTPS, clientName, vehicleModel, armorLevel, quotationAmount, estimatedMargin, daysInProduction - status (ProjectStatus), plant, linkedBOMId, stages[] (ProjectStage per status), costs[], documents[] - **ProjectCost** — type (material|mano_obra|gif|servicio_tercero), amount, currency - **GAP** (Implementation Gap): - type, priority, status, area, description, owner, dueDate, resolution - **Decision** — pending/decided architectural decisions - **TechnicalSpecification** (LMAT 2.0) — armor level specs, production processes, linked to BOMs - **ECN** (Engineering Change Notice) — solicitud → analisis → aprobacion → aplicacion → completado, with department approvals - **SupplyKit** — supply/cutting kit with items, scanned tracking, backorder flag - **PlantRoute**, **CuttingKit** — additional LMAT 2.0 entities for multi-plant routing and cutting operations **AppState:** ```typescript { articles, boms, projects, suppliers, clients, gaps, decisions, checklist, users, auditLog, specifications, ecns, supplyKits, cuttingKits, // LMAT 2.0 currentUser, currentPlant, lastUpdated } ``` **AppAction** — 50+ dispatch actions for CRUD operations on each entity, plus hierarchical BOM operations (MOVE_BOM_COMPONENT, REORDER_BOM_COMPONENTS), mass changes, validations, ECN approvals, etc. --- #### **[store.ts](lib/store.ts)** — State Reducer **Functions:** - `appReducer(state, action)` — handles all 50+ action types with business logic: - Article completeness calculation (weighted fields) - BOM health calculation (% components with completeness >= 80%) - Automatic BOM revision creation on updates with diff detection - Hierarchical component reordering - Mass component substitution across multiple BOMs - Department validation status tracking - Auto-timestamp updates **Key logic:** BOM hierarchical operations use flat storage (parentId/level/order) with tree rebuilding on reads --- #### **[store-provider.tsx](lib/store-provider.tsx)** — React Context Provider **Setup:** - Creates `StoreContext` with `state`, `dispatch`, `resetData` - Uses `useReducer(appReducer, initialState)` - **Persistence**: localStorage with schema versioning (DATA_VERSION = 5) - Validates loaded state against LMAT 2.0 structure; auto-resets on mismatch - Hydration on mount with useEffect, auto-save on state changes - Exports `useStore()` hook **Key pattern:** Client-side state only (no backend); reset data button for demo scenarios --- #### **[constants.ts](lib/constants.ts)** — Lookup Tables & Labels **Mappings:** - `ROLE_LABELS`, `PLANT_LABELS`, `PLANT_CELLS` - `ARTICLE_STATUS_LABELS`, `ARTICLE_STATUS_COLORS`, `ABC_LABELS`, `ABC_COLORS` - `BOM_STATUS_LABELS`, `BOM_STATUS_COLORS`, `BOM_MATURITY_LABELS`, `BOM_MATURITY_COLORS` - `PROJECT_STATUS_LABELS`, `PROJECT_STATUS_COLORS`, `PROJECT_STAGE_ORDER` - `GAP_STATUS_LABELS`, `GAP_STATUS_COLORS`, `GAP_TYPE_LABELS`, `GAP_PRIORITY_LABELS`, `GAP_PRIORITY_COLORS` - `UDM_LABELS`, `ARTICLE_GROUPS`, `VALIDATION_DEPARTMENTS`, `AREAS`, `PRODUCTION_PROCESS_LABELS`, `ECN_STATUS_LABELS`, etc. --- #### **[seed-data.ts](lib/seed-data.ts)** — Initial Demo Data **Data set includes:** - 10 users (admin, gerentes, key_users, operador, consultor) across 2 plants - 5 suppliers (domestic & international, different currencies) - 5 clients (government, embassies, private) - **30 realistic articles**: steel armor plates (6/8/12mm), ballistic glass (NIII/NIV), Kevlar rolls, fasteners, adhesives, paint, interior trim, hydraulic lines, electrical systems, run-flat inserts, hinges, windows, ancillary parts — with group, pricing, lead times, locations, ABC classification, completeness tracking (15%-100%) - **Multiple BOMs** linked to vehicles with maturity stages and revision history - **Projects** in various stages with costing - **GAPs, Decisions, Checklists** for implementation tracking - **ECNs, Specifications, Supply Kits** for LMAT 2.0 - Helper functions: `makeBOMComponent()`, `makeArticle()`, `createInitialState()` that assemble the full AppState --- #### **[utils.ts](lib/utils.ts)** — Utility Functions - `cn()` — clsx + tailwind-merge for Tailwind class composition - `formatCurrency(amount, currency)` — localizes to Spanish (es-MX) with MXN/USD - `formatDate(date)` — Spanish short date format (e.g., "15 ene 2025") - `formatPercent(value)` — formats to 1 decimal place - `generateId()` — timestamp + random suffix for client-side IDs - `searchMatch(text, query)` — case-insensitive substring search used in filters --- ### 4. HOOKS ([hooks/](hooks/)) - [**use-mobile.ts**](hooks/use-mobile.ts) — Responds to `(max-width: 768px)` breakpoint; returns `true` on mobile - Used for responsive layouts in sidebar, dropdowns - [**use-toast.ts**](hooks/use-toast.ts) — Sonner toast notifications API - Methods: `toast.success()`, `toast.error()`, `toast.promise()`, etc. - Used for feedback on save, delete, import operations --- ### 5. GLOBAL LAYOUT & NAVIGATION #### **[app/layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)** — Root Layout - Wraps entire app in `` - Layout structure: ```jsx
// Left sidebar (collapsible)
// Top bar (role, plant, reset)
{children}
``` - Global styles from `globals.css` - Metadata: "TPS ERP - Sistema de Gestion de Blindaje Vehicular" - Viewport: dark theme color, device-width, scale 1 #### **Navigation Hierarchy** (from [app-sidebar.tsx](components/layout/app-sidebar.tsx)) ``` 📊 Inicio └─ Dashboard → / 🔷 LMAT ├─ Dashboard → /lmat ├─ BOMs → /lmat/boms ├─ Especificaciones → /lmat/especificaciones ├─ ECN → /lmat/ecn ├─ Kits Surtimiento → /lmat/kits ├─ Validacion → /lmat/validacion └─ Where-Used → /lmat/where-used 📦 Articulos ├─ Catalogo → /articulos ├─ Nuevo Articulo → /articulos/nuevo └─ Importar → /articulos/importar 📁 Proyectos ├─ Dashboard → /proyectos └─ Nuevo Proyecto → /proyectos/nuevo ✅ Implementacion ├─ Dashboard → /implementacion ├─ GAPs → /implementacion/gaps └─ Decisiones → /implementacion/decisiones ``` --- ### 6. STATE MANAGEMENT APPROACH **Architecture:** Client-side only, **Context API + useReducer** (no Redux, Zustand, etc.) **Store API:** ```typescript useStore() → { state: AppState, dispatch, resetData } // Typical usage: const { state, dispatch } = useStore() dispatch({ type: "ADD_ARTICLE", payload: articleObject }) dispatch({ type: "UPDATE_BOM", payload: { id, updates, reason } }) // State shape: state.articles[] // all Article entities state.boms[] // all BOM entities with hierarchical components state.projects[] // project traceability state.gaps[] // implementation gaps state.specifications[] // technical specs (LMAT 2.0) state.ecns[] // ECN change notices state.supplyKits[] // supply/cutting kits state.currentUser // logged-in user (switcher in header) state.currentPlant // selected plant filter state.lastUpdated // ISO timestamp of last state update ``` **Persistence:** - Auto-saves to localStorage after each dispatch - Schema versioning (DATA_VERSION=5) ensures compatibility - Reset button forces fresh seed data load **Pages consume store via:** - `useStore()` hook → destructure `state` + `dispatch` - Direct state reads for rendering (articles table, BOM tree, etc.) - Dispatch calls for mutations (add, update, delete operations) - No async/async thunks; all operations are synchronous **Hierarchical BOM Operations:** - Flat storage: each BOMComponent has `parentId`, `order`, `level` - Tree building on read via `buildTree()` helper in bom-tree.tsx - Reordering via `MOVE_BOM_COMPONENT` action - Automatic revision creation on significant updates --- ## COMPLETE ROUTE PATHS FOR RECREATION (SvelteKit Structure) ``` / — Home/Dashboard /articulos — Articles Catalog /articulos/nuevo — Create Article /articulos/importar — Import Articles (CSV) /articulos/[id] — Article Detail /lmat — LMAT Dashboard /lmat/boms — BOMs List /lmat/boms/nuevo — Create BOM /lmat/boms/[id] — BOM Detail & Edit /lmat/especificaciones — Technical Specifications /lmat/especificaciones/[id] — Specification Detail /lmat/ecn — ECNs (Engineering Changes) /lmat/ecn/[id] — ECN Detail /lmat/kits — Supply Kits /lmat/kits/[id] — Kit Detail /lmat/validacion — Department Validation /lmat/where-used — Where-Used Analysis /proyectos — Projects Dashboard /proyectos/nuevo — Create Project /proyectos/[id] — Project Detail & Traceability /implementacion — Implementation Dashboard /implementacion/gaps — GAPs List /implementacion/decisiones — Decisions Log ``` --- ## KEY OBSERVATIONS FOR PORTING 1. **State Shape is Large** — 13 entity arrays + currentUser/currentPlant; consider Svelte stores (writable/derived) or a centralized context replacement 2. **Heavy on Business Logic** — completeness %, health %, BOM hierarchy, diff detection, mass changes → encapsulate in shared utilities 3. **Hierarchical UI** — BOM tree with drag-drop is complex; ensure Svelte drag-drop library choice (e.g., svelte-dnd-ghost or similar) 4. **Form-Heavy** — multiple detail pages with complex forms; consider form library (e.g., Superforms + Zod) 5. **Filtering & Sorting** — extensive table operations (articles, projects); reusable filter/sort composables 6. **Localization** — Spanish labels, currency formatting (MXN/USD), date formatting — build i18n support early 7. **Data Persistence** — localStorage strategy needs to transfer; Svelte stores can handle hydration similar to React Context 8. **Component Library** — shadcn-ui → shadcn-svelte (check compatibility of all 40+ components); theme provider needs adaptation 9. **Navigation** — sidebar collapsible state; plant/role switcher in header — easier in SvelteKit with layout groups or stores 10. **No Backend API** — demo app only; plan for API integration later
data.reasoningText:
Perfect! Now I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me compile the report based on all this data. I'll organize it as requested with:

1. Full route/page structure

arguments.file_text:
# Port tps-alpha (Next/shadcn) → sigma-tps (SvelteKit/shadcn-svelte)

## Decisiones
- TPS toma raíz `/`; mover índice demos actual a `/demos`
- Estado: clase runes ($state) en .svelte.ts + Context + localStorage (equiv Context+useReducer)
- Layout TPS dedicado en (tps)/+layout.svelte replicando app-sidebar + header de tps-alpha
- Paridad funcional completa (forms, BOM tree drag-drop, filtros, persistencia, seed)
- EXCLUIR sección /implementacion (gaps, decisiones)
- Todo colocado en src/routes/(tps)/
- UI imports: $lib/components/ui/<comp>/index.js
- Icons: @lucide/svelte

## Rutas a crear (bajo (tps), URL real sin prefijo)
- / (dashboard)
- /articulos, /articulos/nuevo, /articulos/importar, /articulos/[id]
- /lmat, /lmat/boms, /lmat/boms/nuevo, /lmat/boms/[id]
- /lmat/especificaciones, /lmat/especificaciones/[id]
- /lmat/ecn, /lmat/ecn/[id], /lmat/kits, /lmat/kits/[id]
- /lmat/validacion, /lmat/where-used
- /proyectos, /proyectos/nuevo, /proyectos/[id]
- (NO implementacion)

## Progreso
- [ ] Fase 1 Foundation: types, constants, utils, store runes, seed-data
- [ ] Fase 2 Layout+componentes: sidebar, header, stat-card, status-badge, bom-tree
- [ ] Fase 3 Páginas
- [ ] Fase 4 Routing: mover demos a /demos
- [ ] Fase 5 check svelte-check / build

data.reasoningText:
I'm setting up the library structure under `src/lib/tps/` with type definitions, constants, utility helpers for formatting, seed data, and a Svelte store. For the store, I'm deciding between porting the reducer logic as class methods with `$state` or wrapping the existing reducer function in a class.

I'll go with keeping `appReducer` as a pure function and creating a `TpsStore` class that manages state with `$state`, includes a `dispatch` method to call the reducer, handles localStorage persistence through `$effect`, and provides Context integration for accessing the store throughout the app.

Starting with the foundation files now.

// --- BOMs ---
function makeBOMComponent(overrides: Partial<BOMComponent> & { id: string; articleId: string }): BOMComponent {
  const art = articles.find(a => a.id === overrides.articleId)
  return {
    articleCode: art?.code || "",
    articleDescription: art?.description || "",
    quantity: 1,
    udm: art?.udmBase || "pieza",
    cell: "Celda 1",
    operation: "",
    notes: "",
    hasCompleteData: (art?.completeness || 0) >= 80,
    // Jerarquia defaults
    parentId: null,
    order: 0,
    level: 0,
    isKit: false,
    ...overrides,
  }
}

// Helper para crear un kit (componente que contiene otros)
function makeKit(id: string, name: string, cell: string, order: number): BOMComponent {
  return {
    id,
    articleId: "",
    articleCode: "",
    articleDescription: name,
    quantity: 1,
    udm: "juego",
    cell,
    operation: "",
    notes: "",
    hasCompleteData: true,
    parentId: null,
    order,
    level: 0,
    isKit: true,
  }
}

function calcHealth(components: BOMComponent[]): number {
  if (components.length === 0) return 0
  const complete = components.filter(c => c.hasCompleteData).length
  return Math.round((complete / components.length) * 100)
}

// LMAT 2.0: Validaciones de departamentos
function makeDeptValidations(status: "pendiente" | "parcial" | "completo"): DepartmentValidation[] {
  return VALIDATION_DEPARTMENTS.map((dept, idx) => ({
    department: dept,
    status: status === "completo" ? "completado" : status === "parcial" && idx < 3 ? "completado" : "pendiente",
    validatedBy: status === "completo" || (status === "parcial" && idx < 3) ? "Roberto Silva" : null,
    validatedAt: status === "completo" || (status === "parcial" && idx < 3) ? "2025-05-01" : null,
    notes: "",
  }))
}

// --- Proyectos ---
function makeStages(currentStatus: ProjectStatus): ProjectStage[] {
  const now = new Date()
  return PROJECT_STAGE_ORDER.map((status, idx) => {
    const currentIdx = PROJECT_STAGE_ORDER.indexOf(currentStatus)
    const isCompleted = idx < currentIdx
    const isActive = idx === currentIdx
    const daysAgo = (currentIdx - idx) * 5
    return {
      name: status,
      label: PROJECT_STATUS_LABELS[status],
      startedAt: isCompleted || isActive ? new Date(now.getTime() - daysAgo * 86400000).toISOString().split("T")[0] : null,
      completedAt: isCompleted ? new Date(now.getTime() - (daysAgo - 3) * 86400000).toISOString().split("T")[0] : null,
      isActive,
      notes: "",
    }
  })
}

// --- BOMs ---
function makeBOMComponent(overrides: Partial<BOMComponent> & { id: string; articleId: string }): BOMComponent {
  const art = articles.find(a => a.id === overrides.articleId)
  return {
    articleCode: art?.code || "",
    articleDescription: art?.description || "",
    quantity: 1,
    udm: art?.udmBase || "pieza",
    cell: "Celda 1",
    operation: "",
    notes: "",
    hasCompleteData: (art?.completeness || 0) >= 80,
    // Jerarquia defaults
    parentId: null,
    order: 0,
    level: 0,
    isKit: false,
    ...overrides,
  }
}

// Helper para crear un kit (componente que contiene otros)
function makeKit(id: string, name: string, cell: string, order: number): BOMComponent {
  return {
    id,
    articleId: "",
    articleCode: "",
    articleDescription: name,
    quantity: 1,
    udm: "juego",
    cell,
    operation: "",
    notes: "",
    hasCompleteData: true,
    parentId: null,
    order,
    level: 0,
    isKit: true,
  }
}

function calcHealth(components: BOMComponent[]): number {
  if (components.length === 0) return 0
  const complete = components.filter(c => c.hasCompleteData).length
  return Math.round((complete / components.length) * 100)
}

// LMAT 2.0: Validaciones de departamentos
function makeDeptValidations(status: "pendiente" | "parcial" | "completo"): DepartmentValidation[] {
  return VALIDATION_DEPARTMENTS.map((dept, idx) => ({
    department: dept,
    status: status === "completo" ? "completado" : status === "parcial" && idx < 3 ? "completado" : "pendiente",
    validatedBy: status === "completo" || (status === "parcial" && idx < 3) ? "Roberto Silva" : null,
    validatedAt: status === "completo" || (status === "parcial" && idx < 3) ? "2025-05-01" : null,
    notes: "",
  }))
}

// --- Proyectos ---
function makeStages(currentStatus: ProjectStatus): ProjectStage[] {
  const now = new Date()
  return PROJECT_STAGE_ORDER.map((status, idx) => {
    const currentIdx = PROJECT_STAGE_ORDER.indexOf(currentStatus)
    const isCompleted = idx < currentIdx
    const isActive = idx === currentIdx
    const daysAgo = (currentIdx - idx) * 5
    return {
      name: status,
      label: PROJECT_STATUS_LABELS[status],
      startedAt: isCompleted || isActive ? new Date(now.getTime() - daysAgo * 86400000).toISOString().split("T")[0] : null,
      completedAt: isCompleted ? new Date(now.getTime() - (daysAgo - 3) * 86400000).toISOString().split("T")[0] : null,
      isActive,
      notes: "",
    }
  })
}

toolRequests.name: create_file

data.reasoningText: Now I'm setting up the store file to manage the runes with their context.

data.content: El patrón `$effect.root` en el constructor puede causar problemas de ciclo de vida. Voy a simplificar persistiendo directamente en `dispatch`/`resetData`:

toolRequests.name: replace_string_in_file

data.reasoningText: I'm realizing that using `$effect.root` in a class constructor creates a cleanup problem since the effect never gets properly destroyed. It's cleaner to handle persistence directly in the dispatch and resetData methods instead, which avoids the lifecycle complications altogether.

data.content: Verifico que la base compile sin errores:
data.reasoningText: Let me check if the foundation builds correctly and look for any compilation errors in the lib/tps files.

data.content: Base lista. Reviso las APIs de los componentes shadcn-svelte que usaré más (badge, card, native-select, table, tabs, progress):
data.reasoningText:
The foundation is solid, so I'm moving on to update the session memory and construct the layout with shared components. I need to familiarize myself with the shadcn-svelte component APIs—particularly Card, Badge, Button, Input, Select, Table, Tabs, Progress, and Separator—so I understand how to import and use them correctly, like whether they're accessed as Card.Root or Card.Header.

I'm realizing the shadcn-svelte conventions are pretty standard: import the component module and access its subcomponents as properties. For the header, I can stick with native select elements since they're simpler and more straightforward. I'll build out the shared components first—status-badge and stat-card—then tackle the layout with the sidebar and header, checking the APIs for badge, card, native-select, table, progress, and tabs as I go.

data.content: Reviso los componentes compartidos y el dashboard para replicarlos:
data.reasoningText: I'm creating a StatusBadge component that accepts a label and colorClass prop, rendering it as a small rounded span with badge styling that mirrors tps-alpha's status-badge implementation. Let me examine the existing tps-alpha components to match the exact styling and structure.

data.content: Reviso cómo se importan los iconos de `@lucide/svelte` en el código existente:
data.reasoningText:
The dashboard will keep the implementation progress card since it's just displaying the seed data that already exists, but I'll remove the links pointing to the `/implementacion` section that we're excluding from the implementation.

Now I'm starting to build out all the Svelte components. I need to establish a consistent approach for handling icons from Lucide, checking how they're imported and used in the existing codebase.

data.reasoningText:
I've confirmed the icon import path works, so now I'm setting up the component structure. I need to build out the stat card, status badge, sidebar, header, and BOM tree components, then wire up the routes.

Before I continue with the components, let me check the bom-tree.tsx file since it has complex drag-and-drop functionality I'll need to port over.

data.content: Ahora construyo los componentes compartidos en Svelte. Primero los simples (status-badge, stat-card) y actualizo memoria:

toolRequests.name: memory
toolRequests.arguments:
{"command": "str_replace", "path": "/memories/session/tps-port.md", "old_str": "## Progreso
- [ ] Fase 1 Foundation: types, constants, utils, store runes, seed-data", "new_str": "## Notas técnicas
- Foundation creada en src/lib/tps/: types.ts, constants.ts, utils.ts, reducer.ts, seed-data.ts, store.svelte.ts (clase TpsStore + setTpsStore/useStore via Context)
- Componentes TPS en src/lib/tps/components/
- Iconos: import X from "@lucide/svelte/icons/kebab-name"
- Badge shadcn no sirve para colorClass → uso StatusBadge custom (span rounded-full)
- native-select: import * as NativeSelect from $lib/components/ui/native-select/index.js → <NativeSelect.Root bind:value> <NativeSelect.Option>
- card: import * as Card. Reuso cn desde $lib/utils
- bom-tree usa text-success/text-warning → mapear a text-emerald-400 / text-chart-4
- Dashboard: quitar links a /implementacion (no se implementa esa sección)
- Tema: usar tokens existentes de sigma (chart-1..5, primary, sidebar). No copiar globals.css de tps-alpha

## Progreso
- [x] Fase 1 Foundation: types, constants, utils, store runes, seed-data"}

toolRequests.name: create_file

data.reasoningText:
I need to verify that the color classes used in the bom-tree component actually exist in the sigma-tps theme—specifically `text-success` and `text-warning`. Since those might not be available, I'm planning to use the existing Tailwind colors instead: `text-emerald-400` for complete status and `text-chart-4` for incomplete, then check if the CSS variables `--success` and `--warning` are defined in sigma-tps.

The real consideration here is that tps-alpha has its own custom dark navy palette defined in globals.css, while sigma-tps uses the design system's theme tokens. The chart colors and primary tokens are standard shadcn utilities that exist in both, so the functionality will work fine—it'll just adopt sigma's visual theme instead of the original navy palette. Since the user emphasized that both projects use shadcn and want the app ported, I'll proceed with sigma's existing theme tokens and can refine the colors later if needed.

Now I'm ready to start building out all the components and pages systematically.

Building TPS-specific components...

data.content: Ahora el árbol BOM con drag & drop. Creo un controlador reactivo y los componentes recursivos:

toolRequests.name: create_file

data.reasoningText:
Checking that the native select component properly handles the `onchange` event, `class` attribute, and `size` prop — the spread of restProps onto the select element confirms these work as expected, and the event handler can access the selected value via `e.currentTarget.value`. Now I'm moving on to building the BOM tree component, which needs to handle recursive rendering of nested structures using Svelte 5's component recursion capabilities.

I'll create a recursive node component and a parent tree component that manages all the state — the expanded set of nodes, drag state (draggedId, dragOverId, dragPosition), and pass these down as a reactive controller object so child nodes can trigger updates through callbacks while maintaining reactivity across the tree.

I'm thinking through how to structure this cleanly: I could define a controller class in a separate `.svelte.ts` file that holds the reactive state using Svelte's $state runes and exposes methods for toggling expanded nodes, handling drag events, and dropping items. The parent component creates an instance of this controller and passes it down to all nodes, so they can access the current drag state and expanded set without prop drilling individual values everywhere.

data.reasoningText:
I'm looking at the scale of this project—nearly 6000 lines across 19 pages—and weighing whether to parallelize with subagents or handle it myself. The risk with subagents is inconsistent Svelte conventions, but doing it all sequentially would take forever. I think the best approach is to port them myself in batches, reading a few pages at a time and converting them to Svelte to maintain consistency throughout.

Actually, I'll be more strategic: I'll personally handle the simpler list and index pages first to establish clear patterns, then delegate the larger detail and form pages to subagents armed with a detailed porting guide that references my completed files as concrete examples. This way I control the conventions early and give subagents solid templates to follow. Let me start with the index pages for articulos, lmat, proyectos, and the various lmat subpages.

data.content: Tengo ejemplos sólidos de cada patrón (índice, listado, detalle simple). Ahora creo una guía de porting detallada y delego las 16 páginas restantes a subagentes en paralelo. Primero la guía:

toolRequests.name: create_file
toolRequests.arguments: {"content": "# Guía de Porting: Next.js (tps-alpha) → SvelteKit (sigma-tps) Portar páginas React/shadcn a Svelte 5/shadcn-svelte con PARIDAD funcional y visual. ## Reglas de conversión (OBLIGATORIAS) ### Origen y destino - Origen Next.js: `/Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app//page.tsx` - Destino Svelte: `/Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)//+page.svelte` ### Imports (cambiar TODOS) - `@/lib/store-provider` → store: `import { useStore } from '$lib/tps/store.svelte.js';` luego `const store = useStore();` y `const state = $derived(store.state);`. Para mutar: `store.dispatch({ type: '...', payload })`. Reset: `store.resetData()`. - `@/lib/constants` → `$lib/tps/constants.js` - `@/lib/types` → `import type { ... } from '$lib/tps/types.js';` - `@/lib/utils` funciones formatCurrency/formatDate/formatPercent/generateId/searchMatch → `$lib/tps/utils.js`. `cn` → `$lib/utils.js`. - `@/components/shared/stat-card` → `import StatCard from '$lib/tps/components/stat-card.svelte';` - `@/components/shared/status-badge` → `import StatusBadge from '$lib/tps/components/status-badge.svelte';` - `@/components/bom/bom-tree` → `import BomTree from '$lib/tps/components/bom-tree.svelte';` - Iconos `lucide-react` → `@lucide/svelte/icons/` (default import, uno por icono). Ej: `import Plus from '@lucide/svelte/icons/plus';`, `ChevronDown`→`chevron-down`, `AlertTriangle`→`triangle-alert`, `CheckCircle2`→`circle-check-big`, `Trash2`→`trash-2`, `X`→`x`, `Save`→`save`, `ArrowLeft`→`arrow-left`. ### Estado y reactividad - `useState(x)` → `let v = $state(x);` - `useMemo(() => expr, deps)` → `const v = $derived(expr);` o `$derived.by(() => { ... return ...; })` si tiene cuerpo. - `state` del store ya es reactivo (runes). Para derivados del store usa `$derived`. ### Routing y params - Página dinámica `[id]`: `import { page } from '$app/state';` luego `const id = $derived(page.params.id);` - Navegación programática `router.push('/x')` → `import { goto } from '$app/navigation';` `goto('/x')`. - `txt` → `txt`. Mantén las MISMAS clases. - `useRouter` de next → eliminar; usar goto. ### JSX → Svelte markup - `className=` → `class=`. Mantén EXACTAMENTE las mismas clases Tailwind. - `{cond && }` → `{#if cond}{/if}` - `{cond ? : }` → `{#if cond}{:else}{/if}` - `arr.map(x => ...)` → `{#each arr as x (x.id)} ... {/each}` - `style={{ width: `${n}%` }}` → `style="width: {n}%"` - `onClick={fn}` → `onclick={fn}`; `onChange={e => setX(e.target.value)}` → en ``/`` → ``, `

data.reasoningText:
I've got good examples to work with now, so I'm creating a porting guide as a session memory file that references these examples, then I'll dispatch subagents to handle the remaining 13 pages. I'm looking at the list of pages still to convert—form pages for creating and importing articles, detail pages, and various index pages across the material and BOM sections.

I need to batch these into subagent tasks and give them clear instructions on the key conversion patterns, especially how SvelteKit handles dynamic route parameters through `page.params` and how to replace router navigation with the `goto` function. Let me write out that guide now.

data.content: Tengo ejemplos y guía. Ahora delego las 16 páginas restantes a 5 subagentes en paralelo, cada uno con la guía y sus páginas asignadas:

{"agentName": "Explore", "description": "Port LMAT index pages", "prompt": "You are porting Next.js pages to SvelteKit. This is a WRITING task — you must create Svelte files.

FIRST: read the porting guide fully: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/lib/tps/PORTING-GUIDE.md
ALSO read these already-ported reference files to match style EXACTLY:
- /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/articulos/+page.svelte
- /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/proyectos/+page.svelte

Then port each of these pages. For EACH: read the Next.js source fully, then create the Svelte destination file with full functional + visual parity following the guide.

1. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/+page.svelte
2. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/boms/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/boms/+page.svelte
3. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/especificaciones/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/especificaciones/+page.svelte
4. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/ecn/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/ecn/+page.svelte
5. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/kits/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/kits/+page.svelte

Do not run builds. After creating all 5 files, report which files you created and any constants/types you referenced that might not exist (so the orchestrator can verify). Keep Spanish text exactly as-is (no accents added)."}

toolRequests.name: runSubagent
toolRequests.arguments:
{"agentName": "Explore", "description": "Port BOM/spec/ecn detail pages", "prompt": "You are porting Next.js pages to SvelteKit. This is a WRITING task — you must create Svelte files.

FIRST: read the porting guide fully: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/lib/tps/PORTING-GUIDE.md
ALSO read these reference files to match style EXACTLY:
- /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/where-used/+page.svelte
- /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/articulos/+page.svelte
- /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/lib/tps/components/bom-tree.svelte (for BomTree usage and props)

These are DETAIL pages with a dynamic [id] route param. Access the id via: `import { page } from '$app/state';` then `const id = $derived(page.params.id);`. Find the entity in store state by id.

Port each page — read the Next.js source FULLY first, then create the Svelte destination with full functional + visual parity per the guide:

1. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/boms/[id]/page.tsx  (781 lines — complex, uses BOMTree component <BomTree components={...} onReorder={(c)=>store.dispatch(...)} />, has tabs, revisions, drag-drop. Port ALL of it.)
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/boms/[id]/+page.svelte
2. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/especificaciones/[id]/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/especificaciones/[id]/+page.svelte
3. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/ecn/[id]/page.tsx
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/lmat/ecn/[id]/+page.svelte

For local component state (expanded tabs, edit mode, form fields) use $state. For derived from store use $derived. For mutations use store.dispatch with the correct action type (see /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/lib/tps/types.ts AppAction union). Do not run builds. After creating all 3 files, report which files you created and note any action types / props you were unsure about. Keep Spanish text exactly as-is."}

Port each page — read the Next.js source FULLY first, then create the Svelte destination with full functional + visual parity per the guide:

- /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/lib/tps/store.svelte.ts

Port each page — read the Next.js source FULLY first, then create the Svelte destination with full functional + visual parity per the guide:

1. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/articulos/nuevo/page.tsx  (FORM page. Use $state for each form field or one $state object. On submit: build Article, store.dispatch({type:'ADD_ARTICLE',payload}), then `import { goto } from '$app/navigation'; goto('/articulos')`. Use generateId from $lib/tps/utils.js for ids.)
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/articulos/nuevo/+page.svelte
2. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/articulos/importar/page.tsx  (CSV importer. Uses FileReader / parsing / store.dispatch IMPORT_ARTICLES. Port all logic.)
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/articulos/importar/+page.svelte
3. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/articulos/[id]/page.tsx  (560 lines — DETAIL page with edit mode, tabs, documents. Dynamic [id]: `import { page } from '$app/state'; const id = $derived(page.params.id);`. Edit via store.dispatch UPDATE_ARTICLE. Port ALL.)
   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/articulos/[id]/+page.svelte

Use $state for local/form state, $derived for store-derived, store.dispatch for mutations, goto for navigation. Do not run builds. After creating all 3 files, report which files you created and any uncertainties. Keep Spanish text exactly as-is."}

   DEST: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/sigma-tps/src/routes/(tps)/proyectos/nuevo/+page.svelte
3. SOURCE: /Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/proyectos/[id]/page.tsx  (DETAIL, dynamic [id]: `import { page } from '$app/state'; const id = $derived(page.params.id);`. Stage pipeline, costs. store.dispatch UPDATE_PROJECT / ADD_PROJECT_COST / UPDATE_PROJECT_STAGE.)

data.reasoningText: I'm organizing the migration into parallel batches by complexity level. Batch A handles the index and form pages across the LMAT module, Batch B tackles the detail pages with more complex components like BomTree, and Batch C appears to be starting another grouping—I'll dispatch these as separate subagents so they can work concurrently, each receiving the guide path and their specific pages to port.

## Archivos creados

### 1. **[lmat/kits/[id]/+page.svelte](lmat/kits/%5Bid%5D/+page.svelte)**
**SOURCE:** `/Users/carlosgarzagarza/Documents/GitHub/tps-alpha/app/lmat/kits/[id]/page.tsx`

- Página de detalle de kit con id dinámico (`page.params.id` → `$derived`)

### 2. **[lmat/validacion/+page.svelte](lmat/validacion/+page.svelte)**

- Grid de 6 botones (un dept cada uno) con estado visual (icons + colores)
- Dialog de validación: select de status + textarea notas
- Dispatch `UPDATE_BOM_VALIDATION` al guardar
- Reference section LMAT 2.0 con descripciones de madurez

- ✅ `SCAN_KIT_ITEM` — payload: `{ kitId: string; itemId: string }`
- ✅ `UPDATE_SUPPLY_KIT` — payload: `{ id: string; updates: Partial<SupplyKit> }`

data.reasoningText: I'm working through the TypeScript type issues with the especificaciones and ecn indexes from the report. The status variable comes in as a string, but I need to cast it to `BOMMaturityStatus` when accessing the typed record objects, since Object.keys returns string[] and TypeScript won't allow direct indexing of the Record with a string type.

data.reasoningText:
I'm realizing that manually transcribing and fixing code from multiple large reports is going to be too expensive token-wise. Instead, I should work directly from the subagent reports even though they have bugs — that's more efficient than reading everything from scratch and rewriting it myself.

Actually, I made a mistake earlier: I specified `agentName: "Explore"` which is read-only, but if I dispatch subagents without that parameter, they'll use the default agent that can actually write files. So I can re-dispatch the remaining 12 pages (kits index, boms/[id], espec/[id], ecn/[id], kits/[id], validacion, articulos/nuevo, importar, articulos/[id], boms/nuevo, proyectos/nuevo, proyectos/[id]) as writing agents in parallel batches, then run svelte-check afterward to catch and fix any errors.

Before I dispatch them, I should update the porting guide with explicit warnings about the Svelte 5 gotchas — no self-closing non-void HTML elements, no `<svelte:component>` syntax, and the correct import paths for Lucide icons — so the writing agents produce cleaner code from the start.

I'm starting to build the kit detail page and need to verify the lucide icon names I'll be using—arrow-left, package, circle-check-big, clock, triangle-alert, truck, user, qr-code, printer, and download.

For the status configuration, I'll keep it as a config map with icon components. For rendering the dynamic status icon, I'll derive both the status config and the icon component as Svelte 5 derived values, then render it directly as a capitalized variable without needing svelte:component. I also need to handle the case where the kit might not be found with a conditional block, and derive the kit, spec, and status config only when needed. I'm deciding to build the card structure manually with native divs using Tailwind classes that match shadcn's styling — border, background, padding, and typography — rather than importing Card components, to keep things simple and avoid prop mismatches. I'll map the card hierarchy (header, content, title, description) to appropriately styled divs, and use StatusBadge or span elements for the status indicator with colors from statusConfig.

For the form elements, I'm using native inputs and textareas with appropriate handlers and classes, and building buttons with shadcn-like styling to maintain visual consistency. The progress bar will be a nested div structure with a background track and animated fill, and I'll use native checkboxes with change handlers for the scan functionality.

Now moving to the kit page itself. I'll compose the full file with the header actions (outline small buttons for Escanear, Imprimir, Exportar), the back button as a ghost icon link, and the main content sections.

For the validation page, I'm creating a snippet for the BOM card to avoid repeating the markup across the three tabs, then implementing tabs with local state and conditional rendering using styled buttons. The dialog will be a simple modal overlay with native elements, and I'm mapping validation statuses to their corresponding lucide icons.

I'll use native select for the validation status dropdown, derive the department stats and BOM lists, and apply StatusBadge for maturity and armor status displays using the color and label mappings.

data.reasoningText:
I'm noticing the BOM detail route doesn't exist in the current setup. The linked BOM in the detail page would break since it points to `/boms/${id}`, but following the routing convention, it should target `/lmat/boms/${id}` instead—even though that detail page hasn't been implemented yet.

Now I need to create the three files carefully, paying attention to Svelte 5 specifics. For the BOM nuevo page, when adding a new component, I need to satisfy the required fields in BOMComponent: parentId, order, level, and isKit. I'll set those to null, the current index, 0, and false respectively. For updateComponent, I'm handling dynamic field updates by spreading the component and casting the result back to BOMComponent to satisfy TypeScript.

On the form inputs, I'm skipping autofocus to avoid accessibility warnings from svelte-check. For the folioTPS field, I'll use an oninput handler to strip non-digits and limit to 4 characters. The quotationAmount will similarly use an oninput handler to parse the numeric value.

Writing the component files...

For the default plant, I'll read directly from the store's current state during initialization. The matching articles list will be computed with $derived.by, filtering based on search query length. I'm also setting up errors as a reactive state array.

On the detail page, I need to handle the case where a project might not be found, so I'll guard the derived computations—the costs breakdown, current stage index, and linked BOM all depend on the project existing first.

Now I'm mapping out the icon names for each page and writing the three file creation calls. For the updateComponent function, I'm using a type cast to ensure the components array maintains its BOMComponent type after the spread operation.

I'm defining addComponent to find the article, check for duplicates, populate the new component with default values from the plant configuration, and reset the search state. For matchingArticles, I'm filtering articles by code and description when the search query is at least two characters long, limiting results to eight items. The handleSave function will redirect to the BOM list after saving, following the task requirements.

data.reasoningText:
I need to verify the store import path and check how it's being used across the codebase, particularly in the boms listing page to understand the patterns for StatusBadge and maturityStatus implementation.

Now I'm mapping out the lucide icon names I'll need for the three files, making sure they match the actual @lucide/svelte library names like arrow-left, git-branch, list-tree, boxes, triangle-alert, circle-check-big, and the others for the various UI elements and status indicators. Now I'm determining which icons are actually used in the component to avoid importing unused ones. Clock isn't referenced anywhere since getValidationIcon uses Clock3 instead, so I'll skip that. For the validation status display, I'm creating a helper function that maps each status to its corresponding icon component and color class, then I'll use that in the markup to render the icon with the right styling. Now I'm checking how Record indexing works across the different status lookups. The BOM and ECN pages use typed status enums to index into label and color maps, which is safe. For the select elements, I need to handle the value binding and onChange handler — in Svelte I can set the value attribute directly on the select and cast the event value back to the proper status type when dispatching the change.

For the bom object itself, since it's derived from finding the item in the store by id, I'll use `$derived` to keep it reactive. The local UI states like viewMode and showRevisions stay as regular state variables, while selectedRevision becomes a derived value that depends on viewingRevision and the bom's revisions array. The computeDiff function remains a pure utility function, and I need to handle the "not found" case in Svelte's template logic rather than an early return.

Since several derived values like cells and componentsByCell depend on bom existing, I'll guard them with conditional checks—either checking if bom exists before accessing its properties, or using non-null assertions inside handlers that only run when bom is present. I'm setting up derived values for cells, componentsByCell, unassigned components, related ECNs, and the specification, all with proper guards for when bom might be undefined.

Defining derived values with guards... Setting the BomTree to read-only when the BOM status is approved or obsolete, and converting inline styles to Svelte syntax. For the spec page, I'm applying the maturity color border trick directly in the class binding, storing icon components in the tabs array and rendering them with Svelte's const directive. For the loops, I'm using index-based keys for specialComponents and protectionByZone, and being careful about variable naming when mapping over linkedBOMs to avoid collision with the outer bom reference.

Now moving to the ECN page where I'm setting up approvalNotes as a reactive state object, deriving the current ECN from the state, and computing affected BOMs. I'm skipping affectedArticles since it's not rendered and would trigger an unused variable warning. For the approval notes input, I'm binding the value to the department-specific note and updating the entire approvalNotes object on input change.

I'm guarding against a missing ECN with a conditional block, and within the else branch I'm deriving affectedBOMs, canApply, and currentStepIndex—all checking if ecn exists first. The workflowSteps array stays as a constant defined outside since it doesn't depend on reactive data. For the status icon helper, I'm creating a function that returns both an icon component and a Tailwind class based on the approval status.

Now I'm ready to write out all three files, being careful with the select elements and iterating through status labels.

I'm using each blocks to loop through the BOM status entries and the computed diff rows with proper keys. For the comparison footer, I'm replacing the IIFE with Svelte's @const blocks to extract the diff calculations and counts inline. Since computeDiff gets called multiple times in the markup, I'll keep those calls as-is. For type safety with bom potentially being undefined, I'm relying on Svelte's type narrowing within the else branch, though I might wrap the main content in an explicit {#if bom} check to be safe.

Now I'm structuring the template with two separate conditional blocks—one for the not-found state and another for the main content where bom is guaranteed to be narrowed to the BOM type. The derived helpers like componentsByCell will stay at the script level with optional chaining guards. For the health percent bar color, I'm using a ternary expression directly in the class binding to switch between primary, chart-4, and destructive colors based on the threshold values.

For the department validation grid, I'm iterating through each department and using @const to extract the validation record, determine its status, and look up the corresponding icon component. The grid cell gets conditional styling based on whether it's completado (emerald), rechazado (destructive), or pending (secondary), and the icon color matches accordingly. I'm also defining a helper function that maps the status string to the right icon component—CircleCheckBig for completado, CircleX for rechazado, Lock for bloqueado, and Clock as the default.

Now I'm compiling the full list of icons needed across the BOM component to make sure I import everything: ArrowLeft, Plus, ShieldCheck, Users, ExternalLink, CircleCheckBig, CircleX, Lock, Clock, FileWarning, ListTree, List, Boxes, GitBranch, History, TriangleAlert, ChevronRight, Eye, and X. I've verified each one is actually used somewhere in the component.

For the diff display in the revision changelog, I'm using status classes like bg-success and text-success to color the added/removed/modified entries, but I need to check whether the sigma theme actually has these tokens or if I need to map them to the emerald and chart-4 colors like I did for the bom-tree component.

data.reasoningText:
I'm realizing the Article type in sigma-tps requires all the LMAT 2.0 fields, but the Next.js build process creates a simpler partial shape. When I construct the Article object in Svelte with TypeScript, I'll need to either satisfy all required fields or use a type cast to avoid errors. Since the reducer's calcArticleCompleteness depends on the article structure, I should build out the full object with sensible defaults for the LMAT 2.0 fields like descriptionGeneric to maintain parity and keep TypeScript happy.

Now I'm going to write the three files, starting with the `nuevo` page. Building the form fields with bind:value for reactive updates. For the inspectionRequired boolean, I'll use a select that converts the string value back to boolean on change. For numeric inputs like price and leadTimeDays, bind:value with type="number" handles the conversion automatically, though it'll display "0" instead of empty — acceptable tradeoff for simplicity and idiomatic Svelte.

I'll add a scoped `<style>` block to define the `.form-input` class and its focus state, keeping the markup clean. Now I'm thinking through the [id] page structure — I could use Svelte snippets to avoid repeating the field row markup, but since each row has conditional editing logic and different input types, I'll likely write the rows more directly or use a minimal snippet wrapper for the display-only sections. Setting up the reactive state with derived article, editing flag, and form object, then defining helper functions for edit operations and field management. I need to be careful with the optional article reference in val() and setField() helpers, using optional chaining and type assertions where needed. For the icons, I'll only import what's actually rendered in the markup—ArrowLeft, Save, Trash2, AlertTriangle, CheckCircle2, FileText, Image, Upload, Plus, and ExternalLink.

Now handling the icon names in kebab-case and making sure Image is imported as ImageIcon to avoid conflicts with the global. For the where-used links, I need to adjust the paths to match the sigma routing structure at `/lmat/boms/${bom.id}`. The boolean field for requiresLotSerial needs to be converted to a string value for the select element.

For the select bindings, I'm avoiding warnings by using an onchange handler with the selected attribute on each option rather than trying to bind directly. This pattern works cleanly for boolean toggles — setting the selected state based on the current value and converting the string back to boolean on change. I'll apply this same approach uniformly across all edit-mode selects to keep things consistent, though for the string-based selects like UDM labels it might be verbose. The simpler approach is to initialize a form object with the full article data on edit start, then bind directly to those form fields. I'm running into a type mismatch: the form is typed as `Partial<Article>`, so `form.udmBase` expects a `UdM` enum value, but the select options are strings from `Object.entries`. I could cast form to `Record<string, any>` to avoid type errors, but that loses type safety. I'll stick with `Partial<Article>` and handle the enum selects by binding directly—Svelte should handle the string-to-enum coercion at runtime, and if there's a type complaint I can suppress it locally rather than loosening the whole form type.

Actually, for pragmatism I'll just declare form as `Record<string, any>` since there are many enum selects and it avoids cascading type errors. When I dispatch, passing `Record<string, any>` to `Partial<Article>` works fine because `any` is assignable. Now I'm handling boolean selects with a conditional to convert between the boolean value and string representation for the select element.

For the markup, I'm thinking about using snippets to reduce repetition across the display and edit rows. I'll create a wrapper snippet that handles the common structure, then each field can conditionally render either the edit input or display value inside that wrapper. Writing the component files...

Añadí los campos requeridos por el tipo `BOMComponent` de sigma (`parentId`, `order`, `level`, `isKit`) y los campos LMAT 2.0 de `BOM` (`maturityStatus`, `departmentValidations`, `specificationId`, `plantRoutes`, `cuttingKitIds`).
- Proyecto nuevo: formulario con validación de folio, `ADD_PROJECT` + `ADD_AUDIT_LOG`, luego `goto('/proyectos')`.
- Proyecto detalle: dinámico con `page.params.id`, timeline, desglose de costos, estado de resultados, BOM vinculado, documentos y detalle de costos (la fuente original es de solo lectura; no dispara mutaciones). El enlace al BOM apunta a `/lmat/boms/{id}` para seguir la convención de rutas de SvelteKit.

data.content: Now the detail `[id]` page:

toolRequests.name: create_file

data.reasoningText:
I'm building out the detail page for [id] using reusable field section snippets, where each section wrapper takes a title and body snippet. I'll set up named body snippets for each section's fields, use `/lmat/boms/{bom.id}` for the where-used link, and bind form data as a generic Record for editing, then work through each field row for display.

For display I'll use the article value directly, and for editing I'll bind to the form object. I need to handle different field types carefully—strings and numbers use standard value binds, while boolean selects need selected state plus onchange handlers. I'm deciding to inline the four FieldSection divs directly rather than abstract them with snippets to keep things simpler. I'm also setting up a derived computation that checks for missing required fields by area, and handling the supplier select so that changing it updates both the supplierId and supplierName in the form.

data.content:
Listo. Creé las 3 páginas SvelteKit con paridad completa:

1. [src/routes/(tps)/articulos/nuevo/+page.svelte](src/routes/(tps)/articulos/nuevo/+page.svelte) — Formulario con `$state`, valida código/descripción, construye `Article` completo (campos LMAT 2.0 con defaults), despacha `ADD_ARTICLE` + `ADD_AUDIT_LOG` y `goto(\`/articulos/${article.id}\`)`.
2. [src/routes/(tps)/articulos/importar/+page.svelte](src/routes/(tps)/articulos/importar/+page.svelte) — Importador CSV con `FileReader`, parseo/preview con validación, plantilla descargable, despacha `IMPORT_ARTICLES`.
3. [src/routes/(tps)/articulos/[id]/+page.svelte](src/routes/(tps)/articulos/[id]/+page.svelte) — Detalle con `page.params.id`, modo edición (`bind:value`), barra de completitud, campos faltantes por área, multi-proveedor, documentos, conversiones y where-used; despacha `UPDATE_ARTICLE`/`DELETE_ARTICLE`.

Sin errores de TypeScript ni Svelte. Las únicas advertencias son sugerencias del linter de Tailwind (`min-h-[100px]` → `min-h-25`), mismo patrón que ya usa el archivo de referencia `articulos/+page.svelte`, así que no rompen el build.

Gotchas respetados: un solo `<script lang="ts">`, sin auto-cierre de elementos no-void, sin `<svelte:component>`, nombres reales de iconos lucide (`triangle-alert`, `circle-check-big`, `trash-2`, `external-link`, `badge-check`, etc.), `Image` importado como `ImageIcon`, `bind:value`/`bind:checked`, casts en indexado de Records, `StatusBadge` desde `$lib/tps/components/status-badge.svelte`, y textos en español sin acentos tal cual el original.
Now file 3 — ECN.

data.content:
Listo. Porté completa la aplicación **tps-alpha** (Next.js + shadcn) a **sigma-tps** (SvelteKit + shadcn-svelte), colocalizada en `src/routes/(tps)/`, excluyendo solo la sección de implementación.

## Qué se implementó

**Base compartida** (`src/lib/tps/`):
- [types.ts](src/lib/tps/types.ts), [constants.ts](src/lib/tps/constants.ts), [utils.ts](src/lib/tps/utils.ts), [reducer.ts](src/lib/tps/reducer.ts), [seed-data.ts](src/lib/tps/seed-data.ts) — portados con paridad 1:1 (30 artículos, 5 BOMs, 8 proyectos, ECNs, kits, especificaciones).
- [store.svelte.ts](src/lib/tps/store.svelte.ts) — clase `TpsStore` con runes `$state` + Context + persistencia en `localStorage` (equivalente al Context+useReducer de React).
- Componentes: `StatCard`, `StatusBadge`, y `BomTree` jerárquico con **drag & drop nativo** (controlador reactivo + componente recursivo).

**Layout** ([(tps)/+layout.svelte](src/routes/(tps)/+layout.svelte)): sidebar con grupos Inicio/LMAT/Artículos/Proyectos + header con selector de rol/planta y reset, replicando el original.

**23 páginas** portadas: dashboard, artículos (catálogo, nuevo, importar, detalle), LMAT (dashboard, BOMs+nuevo/detalle/importar/cambio-masivo, especificaciones+detalle, ECN+detalle, kits+detalle, validación, where-used) y proyectos (lista, nuevo, detalle).

**Routing**: la app TPS ocupa la raíz `/`; el índice de demos previo se movió a [/demos](src/routes/demos/+page.svelte).

## Verificación
- `svelte-check`: **0 errores en los archivos TPS** (los 651 restantes son preexistentes del repo, en `(previo)`, `sv-grid`, etc.).
- Smoke test HTTP: todas las rutas devuelven 200 y renderizan su contenido (`/lmat/boms/bom1` → "Toyota Land Cruiser" desde el seed, confirmando store + params dinámicos).

Nota: el `vite build` del repo falla por un import roto preexistente en `(previo)/(no)/(demos)/upload` (`file-drop-zone`), ajeno a este trabajo. Si quieres, lo arreglo aparte.

Un detalle clave que resolví: nombrar una variable `state` (alias de `store.state`) rompe la runa `$state` de Svelte 5 — renombré el alias a `app` en todas las páginas.
